response.justification: Sarah has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and the Green Map GIS application, and demonstrated distributed/high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby, but the core requirement for this role is strong modern C++ or Rust, and her C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project. Her current and recent trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling, with no routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or real-time traffic data integration. The skills gap on the primary technical requirement is too large to bridge with adjacent experience.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development (GISCollective, Open Green Map GIS app) shows genuine domain familiarity with geo-data pipelines
High-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O) demonstrates relevant scalability thinking
Consistent TDD/quality-engineering discipline across multiple roles aligns with the role's rigorous QA emphasis
concerns: C++ experience is a single six-month role from 2014–2015 and a university thesis; no Rust experience listed; primary languages are TypeScript and DLang
No routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or real-time traffic integration experience anywhere in the history
Career trajectory since 2018 has moved progressively away from systems/C++ toward web, cloud, and now AI tooling — a significant re-orientation would be required

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation domain experience
